locked_files.reason: Tied to legacy database schema v2.3. Changes will break production payment processing. Contact the payments team before modifying.
CRITICAL: Security configuration managed by DevOps team. Any changes require security review and approval ticket SEC-XXXX
Encryption algorithm tied to compliance requirements (PCI-DSS)
Key rotation period mandated by company policy
Max login attempts set by security team to prevent brute force
Token validation must match auth server exactly. Changes will break all client authentication
Order validation implements 47 business rules. Last changed in Q2 2024 after 3-month testing cycle. DO NOT MODIFY without running full test suite.
Tax calculation uses Avalara API integration. Credentials and endpoint configuration managed by finance team.
Payment processing uses Stripe API v2024.10. Changes require PCI compliance review.

privacy.reason: Database credential - never log or include in error messages
Database credential - never log or include in error messages
Customer identifiers linked to purchase history — PII under GDPR
Email address is PII under GDPR - never log the recipient address
Phone number is PII - never log the destination number
Output contains customer shipping address and contact details (PII)
PCI-DSS cardholder data - never log or expose in suggestions
PCI-DSS cardholder data - never log or expose in suggestions

core.note: This is a security manager. Any single-line change can compromise the entire project.
Reservation logic handles concurrent requests via optimistic locking. Took 18 months to get right under high load — do not refactor without running the full concurrency test suite.
Must be called as the exact inverse of reserveStock. Pair changes to both methods together.

performance.constraint: HFT-level requirements: O(1) processing time expected. No database lookups in processing loop.
O(1) lookup required. Must complete in <2ms p99. No database calls permitted; reads from in-memory cache only.
Must process 10 000 SKU updates/second. O(n) acceptable; O(n log n) only if unavoidable; O(n²) is forbidden.
Must complete in <5ms p99. Called on every cart update.

contract.reason: Signature locked by OpenAPI v2 contract. checkout-service and mobile-app bind to this exact signature. A type change is a breaking API change.
Promotions-service depends on this exact method signature for its async price-adjustment events. Changing parameter types would break the event deserialization.
B2B portal contract v1.2 — the List<Map<String,Object>> structure is serialized directly to JSON. Changing the return type breaks portal parsing.

draft.instructions: Implement email sending via SMTP and push notifications via FCM. Ensure retry logic and rate limiting are applied.
Implement support for new crypto payments without breaking legacy flow.
Implement email sending using JavaMail API or similar. Include HTML template support and attachment handling. Add retry logic for transient failures (max 3 retries with exponential backoff).
Implement SMS sending via Twilio or AWS SNS. Include phone number validation. Handle rate limiting (max 10 SMS per minute per user).
Implement push notification using Firebase Cloud Messaging. Support both Android and iOS. Include notification payload customization.
Implement a notification queue using a BlockingQueue or similar structure. Support batch processing and priority levels (LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL).
Implement delivery status tracking. Return status: PENDING, SENT, DELIVERED, FAILED. Include timestamp and error message if failed.

Implement discount calculation supporting: percentage discounts, fixed amount discounts, buy-one-get-one-free, and tiered discounts based on cart value. Apply maximum one discount per order unless overridden by admin.

Implement order status workflow: CREATED -> PAYMENT_PENDING -> PAYMENT_CONFIRMED -> PROCESSING -> SHIPPED -> DELIVERED. Support status history tracking with timestamps. Allow cancellation only before SHIPPED status.
Implement order search with filters: date range, status, customer ID, minimum/maximum amount. Support pagination (default 20 items per page). Return results sorted by creation date descending.

Generate order confirmation email content including: order summary, itemized list, shipping address, estimated delivery date, and customer support contact information. Support HTML and plain text formats.
Implement payment execution specific to the payment method (credit card, PayPal, cryptocurrency, etc.). Return transaction ID on success.
Validate payment method specific data (card numbers, email addresses, wallet addresses, etc.). Return true if valid, false otherwise.

Implement credit card payment processing via Stripe or similar payment gateway. Include: card tokenization, 3D Secure authentication, and proper error handling for declined cards. Return transaction ID on success.
Implement Luhn algorithm validation for card number, expiry date validation (must be future date), and CVV format check (3-4 digits). Return true only if all validations pass.
